content:
What It Took to Build Save the Flower
When people play a casual mobile game, they usually see only the finished product. They solve
puzzles, complete levels, and move on to the next challenge. What they don’t see is the amount of
work that happens behind the scenes before the game reaches that point.
Save the Flower started with a very simple question:
Can we create a puzzle game that’s easy to understand but still fun enough to keep players
coming back?
That question became the foundation of our project.
The First Prototype
The first version of the game was far from perfect. The core mechanic—drawing a line to protect the
flower from bees—worked, but it didn’t always feel natural. Sometimes the line wasn’t accurate, the
bees behaved unpredictably, and some levels were either too easy or almost impossible to
complete. We kept testing and refining until the gameplay felt right.
Finding the Right Balance
Every level had to feel different. We redesigned puzzles, adjusted bee movement, and refined the
difficulty through repeated internal playtesting to create a satisfying experience.
Paying Attention to the Details
We polished the UI, improved animations, optimized performance, and fixed bugs that only
appeared after long testing sessions. These small improvements made a big difference to the final
game.
What We Learned
Building Save the Flower reminded us that great games are built through patience, iteration, and
teamwork. Every improvement taught us something new.
Looking Ahead
With Save the Flower complete, we’re excited to begin our next projects while carrying forward
everything we’ve learned. Thank you for supporting GameGenesis.
— Team GameGenesis
